description_long: This bungalow features a prominent green gabled roof and a combination of stone and light-colored siding on its exterior walls. The front facade includes a covered porch with a curved railing and a set of steps leading up to the entrance. Multiple rectangular windows are distributed asymmetrically across all sides, with some grouped and others spaced apart. The structure sits on a grassy plot with paved walkways and a small rear patio area. The roofline is complex, with intersecting gables and varying heights, adding architectural interest. The overall appearance is that of a spacious, two-story residential home with traditional design elements.
description: A two-story bungalow with a green gabled roof, stone and light siding, a covered front porch, and asymmetrical window placement.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch with curved railing, and steps leading up to the door.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long green roof, stone siding, and several windows along the length of the house.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a small patio, a secondary entrance, and a mix of stone and light siding with fewer windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended roof, additional windows, and the transition between stone and light siding.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped and spaced windows
